title: Corrupted Packet
story: [Meridian-7 Log #019] The communication channel is unstable. Some data packets were corrupted during transmission. You need to extract valid IPv4 addresses — four groups of 0-255 numbers separated by dots. Corrupted data contains out-of-range values that must be precisely filtered. Meridian-7's network module needs these addresses to establish relay links.
promptChallenge: [Target] Valid IPv4 addresses — each segment must stay within 0–255.
hints: [Hint] This is one of the most classic challenges in regex
promptHints: Tell the AI the exact format of IPv4 and the numeric range of each segment
Hint the AI to handle different numeric ranges segment by segment
Use few-shot examples: 192.168.1.1 is valid, 256.1.1.1 is not

feedback.onPass: [Pattern Accepted] Corrupted data filtered. Only valid addresses remain.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Filtering failed. Numbers above 255 are not valid addresses.
feedback.onPerfect: [Decryption Success] Perfect filtering. Range validation precise to every segment.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual packet repair. IPv4 is just basic work for you.
